initial_meeting: ^State audit at 4 AM? You regulatory people have interesting schedules.
^There have been concerns about this facility

c-0: ^You: Just doing my job, Mr. Chen.
c-1: ^You: I apologize for the inconvenience. I know this is unexpected.
c-2: ^You: There have been concerns about this facility. I need to conduct a thorough review.

chen_professional_response: ^Right. Well, I run a tight ship here despite our budget constraints.
^Whatever boxes you need checked, let's get it done quickly—we have a facility to operate.
chen_apologetic_response: ^I appreciate that. Look, I know you're doing your job.
^It's just... we're understaffed, underfunded, and now I've got surprise inspections at dawn.

c-0: ^You: I understand the pressure you're under. I'll be as efficient as possible.

chen_defensive_response: ^Concerns? We passed our last three inspections with flying colors.
^Our safety record is spotless. Who's been talking?
^It's routine, Mr. Chen. May I see employee records?
^I need to be frank with you about something

c-0: ^You: Just routine procedure. May I see your employee records?
c-1: ^You: Actually, I should be frank with you about why I'm really here.

access_request: ^Employee records? Fine. But I want to know what you're looking for.
access_request_reluctant: ^Fine. But this better be routine. I've got 47 operators keeping 240,000 people supplied with clean water.
maintenance_question: ^Maintenance? We had OptiGrid Solutions in earlier this week for control system upgrades.
^Routine stuff, all contracted properly. Background checks passed.
^OptiGrid Solutions? Can I see their access logs?

c-0: ^You: I'd like to review those access logs if possible.

optigrid_interest: ^Sure, I can pull those. They checked out—proper credentials.

c-1: ^You: Actually, there's something important you should know.

contractors_inquiry: ^Just OptiGrid this month. We've had budget cuts—only essential maintenance.
^That's why this surprise audit is... frustrating. We're doing our best with limited resources.
concerns_question: ^Unusual activity? Not that I've noticed. Why?

c-0: ^You: Standard question. Part of the inspection process.
c-1: ^You: I think we should have a private conversation about something.

chen_cooperation_gained: ^Employee records, maintenance logs, facility access—I'll get you whatever you need.
chen_provides_access: ^Here's a facility keycard—Level 1 access. That'll get you into most areas.
^Restricted zones like the server room need higher clearance, but for an inspection you should be fine.
^Thank you. I'll start with employee records

c-0: ^You: Thank you. I'll start reviewing employee records.
c-1: ^You: I appreciate your cooperation, Mr. Chen.
c-2: ^You: Before I start—about those OptiGrid technicians. I need the full details.

optigrid_details_request: ^Three technicians, here for two days. Network infrastructure maintenance and SCADA optimization.
^They had all the right paperwork. What's your concern?
^I think we should talk about what's really happening here
initial_meeting_end_professional: ^Let me know if you need anything else. I'll be in the Control Room monitoring systems.
initial_meeting_end_grateful: ^Of course. And look... if you do find anything, let me know.
^This facility is my responsibility. These people depend on us.
early_reveal_opportunity: ^Alright, you've got my attention. What's this really about?

c-1: ^You: Nothing. Just being cautious. Let's continue the inspection.

chen_early_reveal: ^You: Mr. Chen, I'm not actually a state auditor.
^You: I'm with SAFETYNET. We have intelligence that ENTROPY operatives have infiltrated your facility.
^You: They're planning an attack on your water treatment systems.

c-0: ^You: Completely serious. At least three operatives targeting your chemical dosing systems.
c-1: ^You: Those OptiGrid technicians you mentioned? That was them. They weren't contractors.

chen_processes_threat: ^I'm working to stop it, but I need your help

c-0: ^You: Our intelligence shows an attack scheduled for 0800 hours.
c-1: ^You: I'm working to identify and stop the attack. But I need your help.

chen_optigrid_realization: ^I... I let them in. I signed off on their access.
^They had proper credentials, background checks... Oh God, what have I done?
^You had no way of knowing. We need to act now
^Don't blame yourself. Help me stop them

c-0: ^You: You had no way of knowing. Their credentials were forged. Focus on stopping them now.
c-1: ^You: This isn't your fault. Help me stop them—that's what matters.

chen_timeline_reaction: ^That's less than four hours from now.
chen_commits_to_helping: ^400,000 people drink this water. We're stopping this.
^I'll pull up all the access logs and SCADA monitoring data.
^Meet me in the Control Room. We'll find what they did to my systems.
chen_maintains_cover: ^Alright... well, you know where to find me if you need something.

b:
^I've been monitoring the systems. You were right—something's wrong.

^Look at these chemical dosing parameters. They shouldn't be changing like this.

^Can I help you with something?

^These are the SCADA monitoring systems—they control the whole facility.

c-1: ^You: Walk me through what I'm seeing.

b: ^Those dosing rates shouldn't be changing outside of manual input from this terminal.
^Someone's got remote access to the system.

^You see something?

^Those parameters have been drifting for the past two days. My operators thought it was sensor issues.

scada_examination: ^This isn't sensor drift. The system is compromised
^Mr. Chen, I need to tell you something

c-1: ^You: Mr. Chen, I need to tell you something important.

scada_technical_explanation: ^Normal chlorine dosing: 0.5 to 1.0 parts per million for disinfection.
^These readings show gradual increases programmed over the past 48 hours.
^If this continues to the levels they're targeting... it would be catastrophic.
^Can you tell what they're trying to do?

b: ^The attack you mentioned—this is it, isn't it?
^They're setting up a mass contamination event.

chen_realizes_threat: ^Can you tell what they're trying to do?
chen_mission_reveal_forced: ^You: I'm not a state auditor. I'm with SAFETYNET.
^You: ENTROPY operatives have infiltrated your facility. They're planning to weaponize your chemical dosing systems.
^My God. How long do we have?
^I'm working to identify and stop the attack

c-0: ^You: Our intelligence shows execution at 0800 hours.
c-1: ^You: I'm working to stop it. But I need your help.

chen_technical_analysis: ^They're increasing chlorine dosing rates while decreasing pH adjustment.
^If this continues to the levels they've programmed... toxic byproducts in the treatment process.
^The contamination wouldn't be immediate—it would build up over hours.
^By the time anyone noticed, thousands would have consumed it.
^I need to find how they're accessing the system
chen_asks_how_to_stop: ^I could try manual override, but if they've corrupted the automation system...
^We could trigger fail-safes. Or worse—trigger the attack early.
^We need to find their control mechanism and disable it properly.
chen_override_risks: ^Too risky. If they've set up trigger mechanisms, a crude override could accelerate the attack.
^We need to find their control point and disable it methodically.
chen_suggests_server_room: ^The server room. If they're accessing SCADA remotely, it's through our network infrastructure.

b:
^You probably already have Level 2 access from... whoever you encountered.

^Be careful—there may be more of them.

chen_suggests_server_room: ^Thank you, Mr. Chen. I'll be careful

c-0: ^You: I'll handle it. Stay here and monitor the systems.

scada_conversation_end_determined: ^Call me if you need technical support—I know every system in this facility.
scada_conversation_end_grateful: ^And... thank you. For taking this seriously. For protecting my people.
